points.text: Crescent Biopharma advanced its lead program CR-001, a PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody, in the ASCEND Phase 1/2 global trial for multiple solid tumor types.

OPKO Health's ModeX Therapeutics initiated a Phase 1 clinical trial for MDX2003, a first-in-class tetraspecific T-cell engager targeting CD19, CD20, CD3, and CD28 for relapsed or refractory B-cell lymphoma.
Biomolecular Holdings secured USPTO Patent No. 12,612,611 for Tetrahedral Antibody technology, a tetravalent bispecific configuration combining two IgG Fc domains with four Fab domains.
Biomolecular Holdings' anti-CD19/CD20 Tetrahedral Antibody (bispecific) targets resistant/refractory B-cell cancers and B-cell-mediated autoimmune diseases via an 'immune reset' mechanism.
Aclaris Therapeutics announced positive full top-line first-in-human results from a Phase 1a trial of ATI-052, an anti-TSLP/IL-4Rα bispecific antibody, validating its potential best-in-class potency.
ATI-052 demonstrated an estimated half-life of approximately 45 days, enabling a potential dosing interval of up to three months for Th2-driven diseases.
The bispecific antibody ATI-052 achieved complete and sustained inhibition of TSLP-induced and IL-4-induced CCL17 (TARC) in healthy volunteers.
Aclaris Therapeutics is advancing ATI-052 through Phase 1b trials in Asthma and Atopic Dermatitis with topline results expected in the second half of 2026.
Aclaris plans to initiate a Phase 2b program for the anti-TSLP/IL-4Rα bispecific antibody ATI-052 in the fourth quarter of 2026.
WuXi Biologics received South Korean GMP certification for three facilities enabling commercial manufacturing of a bispecific antibody for biliary tract cancer.
The Bispecifics Network initiative launched across CancerNetwork, AJMC, and Pharmacy Times to educate stakeholders on bispecific antibody applications in oncology.
Ralph V. Boccia, MD, highlighted the current uptake of bispecific antibodies in hematologic malignancies and their expanding role in solid tumors including small cell lung cancer.
BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies have emerged as a significant treatment modality for early-relapse multiple myeloma, with evolving paradigms driven by new Phase 3 data and combination strategies.

Experts emphasize that while efficacy of BCMA bispecifics is unprecedented, managing cytokine release syndrome (CRS) and immune effector cell-associated neurotoxicity syndrome (ICANS) remains critical for community adoption.
Prophylactic tocilizumab (Actemra) is recommended to mitigate the incidence of CRS and reduce severity to Grade 1 in patients receiving BCMA-targeted bispecific antibodies.
Crescent Biopharma — CR-001 — Multiple Solid Tumors — ASCEND Phase 1/2 global trial ongoing evaluating PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody in first-line and previously treated patients.

ModeX Therapeutics — MDX2003 — Relapsed or refractory B-cell lymphoma — Phase 1 trial (MDX-2003-101) initiated with first patient dosed; novel tetraspecific antibody targeting CD19/CD20 on B-cells and CD3/CD28 on T-cells to mitigate immune escape.

Biomolecular Holdings — Tetrahedral Antibody (anti-CD19/CD20) — B-cell cancers / Autoimmune diseases — USPTO granted Patent No. 12,612,611 covering tetravalent bispecific architecture; Patent No. 12,473,363 (NK/Monocyte Engagers) issued Nov 2025.

Aclaris Therapeutics — ATI-052 — Th2-driven inflammatory diseases — Phase 1a first-in-human trial met target profile with 45-day half-life and complete CCL17 inhibition; Phase 2b initiation planned Q4 2026.
Aclaris Therapeutics — ATI-052 — Asthma and Atopic Dermatitis — Phase 1b trials ongoing with topline data expected H2 2026.

WuXi Biologics — Bispecific Antibody Manufacturing — Biliary Tract Cancer — Three facilities (Drug Substance Facility 5, Drug Product Facility 2, Drug Product Packaging Center) certified by South Korea's Ministry of Food and Drug Safety following a five-day inspection with no critical findings.

The Bispecifics Network — Educational Initiative — Oncology (Hematologic Malignancies & Solid Tumors) — Launched to address multidisciplinary care needs and adverse effect management for bispecific antibody therapies.
BCMA-directed bispecific antibodies — Multiple Myeloma — Phase 3 data presented at CancerNetwork Training Academy confirms role in second-line therapy for high-risk early-relapse patients.
Crescent Biopharma — Oncology bispecific and ADC development — CR-001 (PD-1 x VEGF bispecific) — Clinical-stage pipeline advancing in solid tumors; Q1 2026 financial results reported.
OPKO Health / ModeX Therapeutics — First-in-class tetraspecific antibody development — MDX2003 — Phase 1 enrollment ongoing; preclinical data presented at 2025 ASH and ESMO 2026 conferences.

Biomolecular Holdings — Commercialization of next-generation bispecific antibody architectures — Tetrahedral Antibody platform (anti-CD19/CD20) — Secured foundational US patents for tetravalent bispecific constructs designed to trigger immune reset in B-cell malignancies.
Aclaris Therapeutics — Bispecific antibody commercialization and Phase 2b expansion — ATI-052 (anti-TSLP/IL-4Rα) — Phase 1a results validated 45-day half-life; Phase 2b program scheduled for Q4 2026.

WuXi Biologics — Expansion of integrated CRDMO model for complex biologics — Bispecific antibody manufacturing — Secured South Korean GMP certification to support commercial-scale production and supply chain efficiency in the Asia-Pacific region.

CancerNetwork / AJMC / Pharmacy Times — Educational platform expansion — The Bispecifics Network — Focused on bispecific antibody adoption in hematologic malignancies and small cell lung cancer; addressing safety profiles and multidisciplinary care strategies.

University of Alabama at Birmingham / Vanderbilt-Ingram Cancer Center / Johns Hopkins Medicine — Clinical utilization strategy for BCMA bispecifics — Early-relapse multiple myeloma — Expert panel convened to define patient selection, toxicity mitigation using prophylactic tocilizumab, and sequencing protocols.
CR-001 — Mechanism: PD-1 x VEGF bispecific antibody — Phase: 1/2 (ASCEND) — Population: Multiple solid tumor types (first-line and previously treated) — Status: Global trial ongoing.

MDX2003 — Mechanism: Tetraspecific T-cell engager (CD19/CD20/CD3/CD28) — Phase: 1 — Population: Adults with relapsed/refractory B-cell lymphoma — Comparator: None (dose-escalation/expansion) — Endpoints: Safety, tolerability, pharmacokinetics, and immune activity.

Anti-CD19/CD20 Tetrahedral Antibody — Mechanism: Tetravalent bispecific configuration with dual Fc domains enabling cooperative binding to NK cells/monocytes — Phase: Preclinical (patent stage) — Population: Resistant/refractory B-cell cancers and B-cell-mediated autoimmune disease — Outcome: Deep B-cell depletion and 'immune reset' potential.

ATI-052 — Mechanism: Dual inhibition of TSLP and IL-4Rα (bispecific antibody) — Phase: 1a (completed), 1b (ongoing) — Population: Healthy volunteers (1a), Asthma/Atopic Dermatitis patients (1b) — Comparator: None stated — Endpoints/Results: 45-day half-life, complete/sustained CCL17 inhibition, dose-proportional PK/PD.

Bispecific Antibodies — Mechanism: Dual-target engagement (specific targets not detailed in source) — Phase: Clinical use in hematologic malignancies and small cell lung cancer — Population: Patients with hematologic malignancies and solid tumors — Results: Slower adaptation noted in small cell lung cancer due to potential adverse effects.

BCMA bispecific antibodies — Mechanism: Dual T-cell engagement targeting BCMA — Phase: 3 (data discussed) — Population: Early-relapse multiple myeloma patients (relapse within 3–4 years post-transplant) — Endpoints/Results: Unprecedented efficacy noted; signature risks include CRS and ICANS, mitigated by prophylactic tocilizumab reducing severity to Grade 1.

Biomolecular Holdings — Intellectual Property Expansion — Tetrahedral Antibody technology — Granted USPTO Patent No. 12,612,611 (April 2026) and Patent No. 12,473,363 (November 2025) covering bispecific NK/Monocyte engagers.

WuXi Biologics — Regulatory milestone for commercial manufacturing — Bispecific antibody platform — Certification enables end-to-end services (drug substance, fill-finish, packaging) for oncology indications, addressing growing demand for complex modalities.
